title: Listening - Part 2
description: You will hear a student called Rachel Hughes talking about her internship experience in a historical archive. For questions 7 – 14, complete the sentences with a word or short phrase.
instructions: You will hear a student called Rachel Hughes talking about her internship experience in a historical archive. For questions 7 – 14, complete the sentences with a word or short phrase.

extracts.context: Rachel Hughes talks about her internship experience in a historical archive.
extracts.audio_script: I first became interested in historical archives when I was [7]10 years old[/7]. We had a school trip to a local heritage museum, and that experience sparked my curiosity about the past. Fast forward to my university years, I was fortunate enough to secure an internship at the city's [8]main archive centre[/8]. I was particularly fascinated by the [9]Victorian era[/9] documents I encountered. The experience taught me to handle fragile documents with [10]great care[/10], something that was quite challenging initially. One of the most interesting parts was helping to prepare an exhibit on [11]early transportation[/11], where I researched the history of [12]railway expansion[/12]. This project gave me valuable experience in [13]cataloguing and preserving[/13] old manuscripts. At the end of my internship, I was awarded the [14]Intern of the Month[/14] title, which I consider a great honour.

dialogue.speaker: rachel
dialogue.text: I first became interested in historical archives when I was 10 years old. We had a school trip to a local heritage museum, and that experience sparked my curiosity about the past. Fast forward to my university years, I was fortunate enough to secure an internship at the city's main archive centre. I was particularly fascinated by the Victorian era documents I encountered. The experience taught me to handle fragile documents with great care, something that was quite challenging initially. One of the most interesting parts was helping to prepare an exhibit on early transportation, where I researched the history of railway expansion. This project gave me valuable experience in cataloguing and preserving old manuscripts. At the end of my internship, I was awarded the Intern of the Month title, which I consider a great honour.
